Module:
Distributed Systems 2  |
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEF22
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
1h30  |
TP:
-
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
4
Teaching Objectives
Deepen distributed algorithms, fault tolerance and data consistency
Study concrete systems: distributed files, object management, quorums, P2P
Recommended Prerequisites
Distributed Systems 1
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Chapter 1: Advanced Distributed Algorithms
Election algorithms (Bully, Ring)
Virtual rings and insertion/removal protocols
Termination detection (algorithms, applications)
Chapter 2: Fault Tolerance
Failure assumptions and fault models
Checkpointing and log-based recovery
Reliable broadcast
Consensus and quorums (Paxos, Raft — overview)
Chapter 3: Data Consistency and Replication
Consistency models (strong, sequential, causal, eventual)
Replication protocols (primary copy, active/passive duplication)
Ordered group communication (reliable multicast, atomic broadcast)
Chapter 4: Distributed Information Management
Distributed objects, distributed virtual memory
Cache management, replication and consistency
Large-scale dissemination (multicast, pub-sub)
Chapter 5: P2P Systems and Quorums
P2P architectures: unstructured vs DHT (Chord, Kademlia)
Quorum systems for tolerance and consistency
Applications: Blockchain, BitTorrent (examples)
Chapter 6: Case Studies & Modern Technologies
Distributed files: NFS, AFS, HDFS
Middleware and frameworks: Zookeeper, Kafka, gRPC
Microservices and service-oriented architecture examples
Assessment
Continuous assessment: 40%
Exam: 60%
Bibliographic References
Rachid Guerraoui and Luis Rodrigues, Introduction to Reliable Distributed Programming, Springer, 2010.
A. S. Tanenbaum, M. van Steen, Distributed Systems - Principles and Paradigms, Prentice Hall, 2002.
Michel Raynal, Synchronisation et état global dans les systèmes répartis, Éditions Eyrolles, 1992.
M. Singhal, N. G. Shivaratri, Advanced Concepts in Operating Systems, McGraw-Hill, 1994.
Ajay D. Kshemkalyani, Mukesh Singhal, Distributed Computing: Principles, Algorithms, and Systems, Cambridge University Press, 2008.
Sukumar Ghosh, Distributed Systems: An Algorithmic Approach, Taylor & Francis Group, 2007.
Nancy Lynch, Distributed Algorithms, Elsevier, 1996.
Nicola Santoro, Design and Analysis of Distributed Algorithms, John Wiley & Sons, 2007.
Weijia Jia and Wanlei Zhou, Distributed Network Systems - From Concepts to Implementations, Springer, 2005.
Richard John Anthony, Systems Programming: Designing and Developing Distributed Applications, Elsevier, 2016.
